title: Tool Wear Cost Calculator
description: Calculates the total cost of tool wear per part, including replacement, downtime, rework, and quality losses, based on industrial engineering standards (ISO 8688, Lean, Six Sigma).

inputs.label: Tool Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Purchase price of a single cutting tool or insert.

businessContext_i18n.en: Purchase price of a single cutting tool or insert.

inputs.label: Tool Life (Parts per Tool)
inputs.businessContext: Average number of parts produced before tool replacement is required.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average number of parts produced before tool replacement is required.

inputs.label: Replacement Time
inputs.businessContext: Time required to replace or index the tool.

businessContext_i18n.en: Time required to replace or index the tool.

inputs.label: Machine Hourly Rate
inputs.businessContext: Fully burdened cost per hour of machine operation (including labor, overhead, depreciation).

businessContext_i18n.en: Fully burdened cost per hour of machine operation (including labor, overhead, depreciation).

inputs.label: Rework Rate due to Wear
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of parts requiring rework due to tool wear, based on Six Sigma defect tracking.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of parts requiring rework due to tool wear, based on Six Sigma defect tracking.

inputs.label: Rework Cost per Part
inputs.businessContext: Average cost to rework a defective part (material, labor, overhead).

businessContext_i18n.en: Average cost to rework a defective part (material, labor, overhead).

inputs.label: Scrap Rate due to Wear
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of parts scrapped due to tool wear beyond tolerance.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of parts scrapped due to tool wear beyond tolerance.

inputs.label: Scrap Material Cost per Part
inputs.businessContext: Cost of raw material lost per scrapped part.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost of raw material lost per scrapped part.

inputs.label: Tool Type
inputs.businessContext: Material of the cutting tool, affects wear rate and cost multipliers.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material of the cutting tool, affects wear rate and cost multipliers.

inputs.label: Coolant Used
inputs.businessContext: Indicates if coolant is used; affects tool life and wear rate.

businessContext_i18n.en: Indicates if coolant is used; affects tool life and wear rate.

rules.message: Tool life must be at least 1 part.

thresholds.alert: Tool wear cost per part exceeds $2.00. Investigate tool life or replacement process.
Scrap rate due to wear exceeds 5%. Immediate process review required.

formulas.name: Tool Cost per Part
formulas.description: Direct tool cost allocated per part.

formulas.name: Downtime Cost per Part
formulas.description: Cost of machine downtime for tool replacement per part.

formulas.name: Rework Cost per Part (Wear)
formulas.description: Expected rework cost due to tool wear.

formulas.name: Scrap Cost per Part (Wear)
formulas.description: Expected scrap cost due to tool wear.

formulas.name: Tool Type Multiplier
formulas.description: Adjustment factor based on tool material (ISO 8688 wear resistance).
formulas.formula: CASE tool_type WHEN 'carbide' THEN 1.0 WHEN 'ceramic' THEN 1.2 WHEN 'cbn' THEN 1.5 WHEN 'diamond' THEN 2.0 WHEN 'hss' THEN 0.8 ELSE 1.0 END

formulas.name: Coolant Adjustment Factor
formulas.description: Reduces effective wear cost if coolant is used (typical 10% improvement).

formulas.name: Total Tool Wear Cost per Part
formulas.description: Primary result: total cost per part attributable to tool wear, adjusted for tool type and coolant.

primary.label: Total Tool Wear Cost per Part
primary.description: Fully burdened cost per part due to tool wear, including replacement, downtime, rework, scrap, and adjustments.

tool_cost_per_part.label: Tool Cost per Part
tool_cost_per_part.description: Direct tool cost allocated per part.

downtime_cost_per_part.label: Downtime Cost per Part
downtime_cost_per_part.description: Machine downtime cost for tool replacement per part.

rework_cost_per_part_wear.label: Rework Cost per Part (Wear)
rework_cost_per_part_wear.description: Expected rework cost due to tool wear.

scrap_cost_per_part_wear.label: Scrap Cost per Part (Wear)
scrap_cost_per_part_wear.description: Expected scrap cost due to tool wear.

tool_type_multiplier.label: Tool Type Multiplier
tool_type_multiplier.description: Adjustment factor based on tool material.

coolant_adjustment.label: Coolant Adjustment Factor
coolant_adjustment.description: Reduction factor if coolant is used.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excessive Replacement Time
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Replacement time above 15 minutes significantly increases downtime cost.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Rework Rate
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Rework rate above 3% indicates tool wear is affecting quality.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Tool Life
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Tool life below 200 parts may indicate suboptimal cutting parameters.

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Cutting Parameters
suggestedActions.description: Review speed, feed, and depth of cut to extend tool life (Lean/Six Sigma approach).

suggestedActions.label: Implement Predictive Maintenance
suggestedActions.description: Use tool wear monitoring sensors to reduce unplanned downtime.

suggestedActions.label: Standardize Tool Replacement Procedure
suggestedActions.description: Reduce replacement time through SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Die) techniques.

suggestedActions.label: Evaluate Coolant System
suggestedActions.description: Ensure coolant flow and concentration are optimal to reduce wear.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Cost
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Primary result adjusted by a confidence factor (0.8 to 1.2) based on input data completeness and variability. Default 1.0 if all inputs provided.
